in effect.explanation_en: basically or essentially, even if not stated exactly that way
in effect.example_sentence: His silence was, in effect, a refusal.
in effect.original_sentence: Everyone I talked to said, in effect, I'd love that.

figure.explanation_en: to think about a problem and find a solution
figure.example_sentence: I need to figure out how to fix this machine.
figure.original_sentence: I continued to think about it and to figure how I could turn the idea into profit.

out of nowhere.example_sentence: The idea came to me out of nowhere while I was sleeping.
out of nowhere.original_sentence: Then one day, as I was driving to work, the answer came out of nowhere.

worn-out.explanation_en: in bad condition because of long use or age
worn-out.example_sentence: He wore his worn-out shoes for the hike.
worn-out.original_sentence: Twenty-two miles from the center of the city, I found a worn-out fifty-acre farm priced at $8,500.

work out.explanation_en: to agree upon or arrange the details of something
work out.example_sentence: We need to work out the details of our travel plan.
work out.original_sentence: I bought it, paying only one-third down and working out a mortgage with the owner for the balance.

prospective.explanation_en: likely to happen or become something in the future
prospective.example_sentence: The company is meeting with prospective clients today.
prospective.original_sentence: I wanted my prospective buyers to see that in a few years, their estate would be covered with beautiful pine trees.

small-scale.explanation_en: involving a small number of people or a small area
small-scale.example_sentence: They started a small-scale business from their garage.
small-scale.original_sentence: I got several mailing lists of young executives in the city and put on a small-scale direct mail campaign.

wholesome.explanation_en: good for your health or moral well-being
wholesome.explanation_zh: 有益健康的，有益身心的
wholesome.example_sentence: The family enjoyed a wholesome meal together.
wholesome.original_sentence: I also described the potentials for recreation and wholesome living.

exposed to.explanation_en: to be in contact with or experience something
exposed to.example_sentence: Students should be exposed to different cultures.
exposed to.original_sentence: I made a nice profit because I let myself be exposed to ideas of other intelligent people.

incorporate.explanation_en: to include something as part of a larger group or system
incorporate.example_sentence: You should incorporate more exercise into your daily routine.
incorporate.original_sentence: There are many ways to get mental stimulation, but here are two that you can incorporate into your pattern of life.

rub shoulders.explanation_en: to meet and spend time with people
rub shoulders.example_sentence: At the conference, you can rub shoulders with industry leaders.
rub shoulders.original_sentence: Rub shoulders and minds with other success-oriented people.

success-oriented.example_sentence: She is a success-oriented person who always sets high goals.
success-oriented.original_sentence: Rub shoulders and minds with other success-oriented people.

pick up.explanation_en: to learn or get something (like an idea or skill) easily
pick up.example_sentence: I picked up a few Spanish phrases while on vacation.
pick up.original_sentence: So often I hear someone say, I picked up a great idea this noon at the blank meeting, or during the meeting yesterday I got to thinking.

undernourished.explanation_en: not having enough food or mental input to be healthy
undernourished.explanation_zh: 营养不良的，缺乏养分的
undernourished.example_sentence: Without new books, his mind felt undernourished.
undernourished.original_sentence: Remember, a mind that feeds only on itself soon is undernourished, becoming weak and incapable of creative progressive thought.

broaden.explanation_en: to make something wider or more inclusive
broaden.example_sentence: Traveling helps to broaden your horizons.
broaden.original_sentence: Association with people who have different job interests broadens your thinking and helps you to see the big picture.

see the big picture.explanation_en: to understand the whole situation rather than just the details
see the big picture.example_sentence: Don't focus on small mistakes; try to see the big picture.
see the big picture.original_sentence: Association with people who have different job interests broadens your thinking and helps you to see the big picture.
